macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: Indian Stock Markets Plunge on Geopolitical Tensions and Rising Oil Prices
items.summary: Sensex plunged over 1,500 points (down ~2%) to 73,023.61 and Nifty50 fell nearly 2% to 22,634.20, wiping out INR 9 lakh crore in investor wealth, driven by US-Iran conflict fears over Strait of Hormuz and Brent crude near $112.94 per barrel.

items.title: Asian Shares Tumble Amid US-Iran Strait of Hormuz Crisis
items.summary: South Korea's Kospi dropped 5%, Japan Nikkei slid 4.8%, Hong Kong Hang Seng lost 2.8%, and Shanghai Composite fell nearly 2%; triggered by Trump's warning to obliterate Iran's power plants if Hormuz not opened, with Iran threatening retaliation.

items.title: US Markets Close Lower on Friday Amid Escalating Global Tensions
items.summary: S&P 500 fell 100.01 points to 6,506.48, Dow dropped 443.96 to 45,577.47, and Nasdaq sank 443.08; Polymarket odds show 56% chance of S&P 500 down on March 23 vs prior close.

items.title: Central Banks Shift Hawkish as Energy Shock Reshapes Rate Expectations
items.summary: All major central banks turned hawkish last week due to inflation risks from energy shock; US rate cuts priced out, hikes expected in Europe, EMs like Chile, Hungary, Mexico, South Africa; Norges Bank to hold rates.

items.title: Indian Indices Open Sharply Lower in Pre-Market
items.summary: Nifty 50 down 1.26% to 22,824.35 and Sensex down 1.07% to 73,732.58 in pre-opening on March 23, reflecting broad sell-off across sectors amid global risk-off mood.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: NYSE Exchanges Remove Position Limits on Crypto Options
items.summary: NYSE Arca and NYSE American have waived the 25,000 contract position limit on options tied to 11 crypto ETFs, with SEC approval allowing immediate implementation. This move is expected to increase institutional participation and liquidity in crypto options markets.

items.title: Bitcoin Falls Below $69,000 Amid Market Decline
items.summary: Bitcoin has dropped below $69,000 with a -0.83% decline, triggering a shift to defensive positioning in derivatives markets. The broader cryptocurrency market is declining, though AI and Meme sectors are rising against the trend.

items.title: AI and Meme Sectors Surge Against Market Decline
items.summary: The AI sector rose 10.36% in 24 hours with Siren (SIREN) surging 163.98%, while the Meme sector gained 3.66% with Banana For Scale (BANANA) rising 48.95%, outperforming the broader market downturn.

items.title: Institutional Investors Show Strong Bullish Sentiment on Crypto
items.summary: 74% of large investors are bullish on cryptocurrency, with 65% citing clearer regulations as the primary driver for increased 2026 purchases. Tokenization interest has grown from 40% to 64% among asset managers.

items.title: SEC Identifies 18 Crypto Tokens as Digital Commodities
items.summary: The SEC has designated 18 crypto assets as digital commodities, marking a broader regulatory shift that clarifies the classification of blockchain-based assets and could reshape market structure.

items.title: OKX Launches MON (Monad) Spot Trading
items.summary: OKX exchange has announced the launch of spot trading for MON (Monad), expanding trading options for the blockchain project.

items.title: Grayscale Submits HYPE ETF S-1 Application
items.summary: Grayscale has filed an S-1 application for its HYPE ETF, though staking features are currently not permitted in the product.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: OpenAI Announces o3 Operator Model Update
items.summary: OpenAI has released an update to its o3 Operator model, featuring enhanced AI performance, improved context understanding, and seamless API integration for better developer use.

items.title: OpenAI Plans to Double Workforce to 8,000 by 2026
items.summary: Defying tech layoff trends, OpenAI aims to nearly double its staff to 8,000 employees by the end of 2026 to support rapid expansion.

items.title: IIT Kanpur Launches Intensive AI and ML Certification Program
items.summary: IIT Kanpur announced a four-week certificate program in AI, ML, Neural Networks, and Deep Learning starting June 15, 2026, open to students, professionals, and researchers with Python-based training.

items.title: Elon Musk's Tesla and SpaceX Announce $20B 'Terafab' Chip Project
items.summary: Tesla and SpaceX revealed plans for 'Terafab', a $20 billion semiconductor facility aimed at advancing AI and computing capabilities.

items.title: University of Sharjah Develops AI Model to Predict High-Risk Drivers
items.summary: Scientists created a machine learning model that flags high-risk motorists before they drive, enhancing road safety through predictive analysis.

items.title: Hitachi Mining Innovation Pitch Selects 10 AI Finalists for 2026
items.summary: Hitachi selected 10 finalists for its 2026 innovation pitch, focusing on AI-powered equipment, machine learning for predictive maintenance, and site-wide optimization in mining.

items.title: Digital Assistant Market Projected to Reach $15.89B in 2026
items.summary: The DA market, driven by AI, NLP, and ML advancements, is expected to grow from $13.22B in 2025 to $15.89B in 2026 at a 20.1% CAGR, with further expansion to $33.35B by 2030.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Generative AI Dominates GDC 2026 Discussions but Faces Pushback
items.summary: AI tools were prominent at the Game Developers Conference, with vendors pitching generative AI for prototyping and assets, but absent from finished games due to gamer and developer concerns over copyright, quality, and backlash. Google promoted Gemini-powered tools, yet no killer use case has emerged.

items.title: Nintendo Plans Up to 25 Million Switch 2 Units by March 2026
items.summary: Nintendo has reportedly instructed suppliers to produce up to 25 million Switch 2 consoles by March 2026, leveraging subsidized pricing for a competitive edge over PS5 and Steam Deck, especially in Japan.

items.title: Savvy Games Group Acquires ByteDance's Moonton Studio for $6 Billion
items.summary: Saudi Arabia's Savvy Games Group, backed by the Public Investment Fund, agreed to buy Mobile Legends: Bang Bang developer Moonton from ByteDance to bolster its mobile gaming and esports presence in Southeast Asia.

items.title: Crimson Desert Tops Steam Charts Amid GenAI Artwork Controversy
items.summary: Crimson Desert leads Steam's top paid games for March 16-22, but developer Pearl Abyss launched a comprehensive audit of in-game assets after players discovered generative AI artwork, sparking criticism.

items.title: NVIDIA DLSS 5 Sparks Backlash Over AI in Gaming
items.summary: NVIDIA's DLSS 5 enhances graphics but faces gamer criticism for promoting 'AI slop' and altering artistic intent, amid ongoing industry layoffs at studios like Ubisoft and Crystal Dynamics.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.
